message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Double Date (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: <100 (very new; TripAdvisor 1 review, Yelp page exists but rating not surfaced in search) [UNVERIFIED exact totals]
- Sentiment: mixed - too early to call; single TripAdvisor review is critical (3.0/5, 'overpriced for average food')
- Longevity: Opened November 12, 2025 - approximately 7 months in operation as of June 2026; operated by Omni Hotels (Omni family ~30 years per chef bio)
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'Double Date Rancho Mirage' direct-name queries and 'Omni Rancho Las Palmas dining'. Surfaces in 'new restaurants Rancho Mirage 2025' via Palm Springs Life and PS Tribune. Does NOT surface in general 'best Italian Rancho Mirage' or 'date night Rancho Mirage 2026' lists - those still default to Norma's, Enzo's, Pinzimini, Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare. Did NOT appear in broader 'new restaurants Coachella Valley Nov 2025 Italian' query.
- Site structured data: Own domain doubledateranchomirage.com exists but is behind Cloudflare bot protection (returns JS challenge to crawlers); schema.org/JSON-LD presence could not be verified via crawl [UNVERIFIED]. Omni's own page at omnihotels.com/.../double-date is the primary canonical entry. OpenTable, Yelp, TripAdvisor, Apple Maps, AAA TripCanvas, VisitGreaterPalmSprings listings exist - good multi-platform coverage typical of resort-backed launch.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Double Date Restaurant Review Summary

**Total Reviews:** 1 review

**Overall Rating:** 3.0 out of 5 bubbles

**The Single Review:**

The one review from January 2026 rates the restaurant poorly, stating: "Overpriced food and not the best quality. The pasta was ok, fish was dense and chicken was undercooked."

The reviewer gave specific category ratings:
- Value: 2/5
- Service: 4/5
- Food: 3/5
- Atmosphere: 3/5

The reviewer concluded that "There are better restaurants for the price in Coachella Valley," suggesting the establishment's value proposition is weak compared to local alternatives.

Links: [{"title":"James Beard Awards – James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/awards"},{"title":"The 2026 James Beard Media Award Nominees | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/stories/james-beard-media-award-nominees-2026"},{"title":"The 2026 James Beard Award Nominees | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/stories/james-beard-awards-restaurant-and-chef-nominees-2026"},{"title":"The 2024 Restaurant and Chef Award Nominees | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/stories/the-2024-restaurant-and-chef-award-nominees"},{"title":"James Beard Awards reveal 2026 semifinalists for standout US chefs and restaurants | kvue.com","url":"https://www.kvue.com/article/news/nation-world/james-beard-semifinalists-list-2026/507-c58835cc-5376-4b50-862f-ce81837d9aa0"},{"title":"James Beard Award Semifinalists 2026 | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/stories/james-beard-award-semifinalists-2026"},{"title":"The 2025 James Beard Restaurant and Chef Award Nominees | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/stories/2025-james-beard-awards-restaurant-and-chef-nominees"},{"title":"The Winners of the 2025 James Beard Awards®","url":"https://blog.resy.com/2025/04/2025-james-beard-finalists/"},{"title":"James Beard Foundation | JBF","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/awards/search-past-awards"}]

Based on the search results, I found a reference to Jose Lopez and "Omni" in relation to the James Beard Awards. Earl James Reynolds was a semifinalist for Best Chef: Mountain at the 2026 James Beard Awards for Herb and Omni in Whitefish, MT.

However, the search results don't provide information about "Jose Lopez" being a nominee or winner specifically for Omni. The results show that nominees were announced on Tuesday, March 31, 2026, with winners to be announced on Monday, June 15, 2026, so if you're looking for the final winner status, that announcement may not have occurred yet at the time of my search or may require additional searching.

Would you like me to search for more specific information about Jose Lopez or perform another search with different keywords?

Links: [{"title":"Double Date in Rancho Mirage, and 5 Other New Restaurants to Try | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/double-date-in-rancho-mirage-and-5-other-new-restaurants-to-try/"},{"title":"DOUBLE DATE, Rancho Mirage - Menu, Prices, Restaurant Reviews & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32942-d33876760-Reviews-Double_Date-Rancho_Mirage_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"DOUBLE DATE - Updated December 2025 - 41000 Bob Hope Dr, Rancho Mirage, California - Italian - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/double-date-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"Home - Double Date Coastal Italian in Rancho Mirage, CA","url":"https://www.doubledateranchomirage.com/"},{"title":"Double Date Italian Table Restaurant - Rancho Mirage, CA","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/double-date-italian-table-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"Restaurant News Bites: A New Farmers' Market in DHS; On the Mark Expanding to Rancho Mirage; and More! - Coachella Valley Independent","url":"https://cvindependent.com/2025/11/restaurant-news-bites-a-new-farmers-market-in-dhs-on-the-mark-expanding-to-rancho-mirage-and-more/"},{"title":"Rancho Mirage, CA 92270 - Michelin Star Restaurant","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Michelin+Star+Restaurant&find_loc=Rancho+Mirage%2C+CA+92270"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurant Week is the Perfect Excuse for a Desert Food Getaway","url":"https://luxebeatmag.com/palm-springs-restaurant-week-is-the-perfect-excuse-for-a-desert-food-getaway/"},{"title":"MICHELIN Guide Restaurants in Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/article/michelin-guide-ceremony/michelin-guide-restaurants-in-palm-springs-ca"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Double Date in Rancho Mirage, though the results don't include specific information about a Michelin Guide feature or Food Network feature. Here's what I found:

Double Date is located at the Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa in Rancho Mirage and is a dining experience by executive chef Jose Lopez, a member of the Omni family for almost 30 years. Double Date is inspired by the Coachella Valley's reputation as the 'date capital of the world,' home to many local date farms that play an important role in the region's agricultural heritage.

The restaurant features an Italian-inspired menu and cocktail program, celebrating bold flavors and shared connection. Double Date combines California sunshine and Italian traditions into dishes like lamb osso buco with date molasses.

Regarding the Michelin and Food Network features specifically mentioned in your query, those details were not found in the search results. If you're looking for more specific information about those features, you may want to check the restaurant's official website or contact them directly.

content.text:
I have enough to make my verification call. Key findings:

1. **Opening date Nov 12, 2025**: CONFIRMED via PS Tribune ("officially opened on November 12" 2025).
2. **Chef tenure ~30 years**: SLIGHTLY CONTRADICTED — PS Tribune says "27-year veteran," not ~30. Omni's marketing language says "almost 30 years." Close but not identical; minor discrepancy.
3. **No credentials**: CONFIRMED — no James Beard, Michelin, Food Network mentions in any source. Independent search of jamesbeard.org returned no Lopez-at-Omni match.
4. **TripAdvisor 1 review at 3.0/5**: CONFIRMED via direct fetch.
5. **Operated by Omni**: CONFIRMED.
6. **~7 months in operation as of June 2026**: CONFIRMED (Nov 12 2025 → June 2026 ≈ 7 months).

confirms: Opened November 12, 2025 (PS Tribune confirms 'officially opened on November 12')
Approximately 7 months in operation as of June 2026
Operated by Omni Hotels at Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa
Credentials: [] — no James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network credentials found for restaurant or Chef Jose Lopez in independent searches
TripAdvisor: 1 review, 3.0/5 rating, critical review citing overpriced/average food (verified via direct fetch)
Multi-platform listing coverage exists (Yelp, TripAdvisor, OpenTable, Apple Maps, VisitGreaterPalmSprings, PS Life, PS Tribune)
Surfaces in 'new restaurants Rancho Mirage 2025' coverage via Palm Springs Life and PS Tribune
contradicts: Chef tenure claim 'Omni family ~30 years': PS Tribune reports Chef Jose Lopez is a '27-year veteran' of Omni, not ~30 years. Omni's own marketing says 'almost 30 years' but the more concrete independent figure is 27. Minor overstatement.
flagged_as_unverified: Yelp review count and rating — Yelp page exists but rating/count could not be surfaced via search snippets or direct fetch
Site structured data / JSON-LD on doubledateranchomirage.com — Cloudflare bot protection blocks crawl, unverifiable
Exact total review volume across all platforms (<100 is plausible given Nov 2025 opening, but not directly counted)
Whether the restaurant appears (or doesn't appear) in 'best Italian Rancho Mirage' or 'date night Rancho Mirage 2026' lists — would require running those specific queries
input.notes: Credentials claim of [] holds up — no James Beard nominations/wins, no Michelin recognition, no Food Network features found for Double Date or Chef Jose Lopez. The opening date (Nov 12, 2025) is independently confirmed by PS Tribune. Only meaningful discrepancy: chef tenure is 27 years per PS Tribune, not 'almost 30' — the Omni website's marketing copy rounds up. The Coachella Valley Independent's Nov 2025 restaurant news bites mention does not appear to feature Double Date prominently (would need full fetch to confirm). Restaurant is genuinely new (~7 months) and credential-free — assessment is accurate.
